industries.name: MRI Contrast Agents
uses: Gadolinium-based contrast agents (GBCAs) are injected intravenously to enhance MRI imaging — Gd³⁺ has 7 unpaired electrons giving it the strongest paramagnetic effect of any stable element, dramatically shortening the T1 relaxation time of nearby water protons and brightening signal

GBCAs in clinical use include gadopentetate dimeglumine (Magnevist), gadobutrol (Gadovist/Gadavist), gadoterate meglumine (Dotarem), and gadoxetic acid (Primovist/Eovist) — used in brain, spine, vascular, cardiac, and liver imaging
Approximately 30 million GBCA-enhanced MRI procedures performed annually worldwide — gadolinium is the most widely administered metal in medicine by number of injections

Safety concerns: gadolinium deposition in brain and other tissues after repeated GBCA exposure has been documented — driving development of macrocyclic agents (more stable chelates) over linear agents and research into gadolinium-free MRI contrast
Gadolinium-based contrast agents chelated as DTPA, DOTA, or BOPTA complexes — free Gd³⁺ is highly toxic; tight chelation is essential for safety

companies.name: Bayer Radiology
companies.use: Largest GBCA manufacturer. Produces Magnevist (linear gadopentetate) and Gadovist (macrocyclic gadobutrol) — combined the most widely used MRI contrast agents globally. Major consumer of gadolinium oxide for pharmaceutical synthesis.

companies.name: Guerbet
companies.use: Major MRI contrast agent manufacturer. Produces Dotarem (gadoterate meglumine, macrocyclic) — considered the safest GBCA due to its macrocyclic structure — and Artirem for cardiac and dynamic MRI applications.

companies.name: GE Healthcare
companies.use: Major GBCA producer. Manufactures Omniscan (gadodiamide, linear) and Clariscan (gadoterate, macrocyclic). Also produces Primovist (gadoxetic acid) for hepatocyte-specific liver MRI with Bayer partnership.

industries.name: Nuclear Reactor Control
uses: Gadolinium-155 and gadolinium-157 have the highest thermal neutron absorption cross-sections of all stable nuclides (61,000 and 255,000 barns respectively) — used as burnable neutron absorbers in nuclear fuel

Gadolinium oxide (Gd₂O₃) mixed into UO₂ nuclear fuel pellets as burnable poison: absorbs excess neutrons at beginning of fuel cycle, then burns away as fuel depletes — allows higher initial fuel enrichment without reactor control problems
Gadolinium shielding in neutron radiography and neutron tomography — thin gadolinium foil or gadolinium-doped scintillators convert neutrons to detectable photons for imaging

Gadolinium neutron capture therapy (GdNCT): experimental cancer treatment — gadolinium compounds concentrate in tumor tissue; thermal neutron irradiation causes nuclear decay products to destroy tumor cells locally

companies.name: Framatome
companies.use: Major nuclear fuel assembly manufacturer incorporating gadolinium oxide as burnable absorber in UO₂ fuel pellets for PWR reactors. Supplies Gd-bearing fuel to EDF French fleet and export customers worldwide.

companies.name: Westinghouse Electric
companies.use: Major nuclear fuel manufacturer using Gd₂O₃ burnable poison in fuel pellets for PWR and BWR reactors. Supplies gadolinium-bearing nuclear fuel to US and international nuclear power plants.

industries.name: Magnetocaloric & Specialty Materials
uses: Gadolinium metal has a Curie temperature of 20°C — at room temperature gadolinium undergoes a large magnetocaloric effect (temperature changes with applied magnetic field). Explored as solid refrigerant in near-room-temperature magnetic refrigeration systems
Gadolinium gallium garnet (GGG, Gd₃Ga₅O₁₂) as substrate for magneto-optical isolator films and as garnet scintillator crystals for PET and gamma detectors
Gadolinium iron garnet (GdIG) for magneto-optical devices, magneto-optical recording research, and microwave ferrite components
Gadolinium orthosilicate (GSO, Gd₂SiO₅:Ce) scintillator crystals for PET scanner detectors — offers fast decay time and good stopping power for 511 keV annihilation photons

companies.name: Saint-Gobain Crystals
companies.use: Produces gadolinium-based scintillator crystals including GSO and GAGG (Gd₃Al₂Ga₃O₁₂:Ce) for PET scanners, SPECT systems, and radiation detection instruments. Major consumer of high-purity gadolinium oxide.
